trigger.reason: OpenAI launched new Codex tools targeting six white-collar verticals (data analytics, creative production, sales, product design, equity investing, investment banking), a high-profile product release likely to trigger sympathy-fade selling across financial software, analytics, and SaaS platforms.

primary_event.headline: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
primary_event.summary: OpenAI released six vertically-targeted plug-ins for Codex aimed at data analytics, creative production, sales, product design, equity investing, and investment banking. Each tool bundles integrations, instructions, and context to approximate a specific job, available within the Codex app.

secondary_events.headline: Anthropic scales Claude Mythos to critical infrastructure in 15+ countries
Microsoft launches Scout, an OpenClaw-inspired personal assistant

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: OpenAI launched six vertically-targeted Codex tools for white-collar work (data analytics, creative, sales, product design, equity investing, investment banking), prompting sympathy-fade selling in financial software, analytics, and SaaS.
discovery.run_summary: The trigger targets white-collar knowledge work verticals (analytics, IB, sales), not municipal services, semis, edge-AI, payroll compliance, or government workflow software. Most candidates in today's basket are caught in thematic 'AI is bad for SaaS' sympathy fades despite their core moats (regulatory compliance, physical infrastructure, multi-year government contracts, semiconductor capex tailwinds) being orthogonal to OpenAI's Codex verticals. Best setups are vertical-workflow software names with deep regulatory/switching-cost moats experiencing clean sympathy drops.

discoveries.name: Blackbaud, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors discuss AI competition generally and BLKB's own AI investments (including agentic AI in their products), but the nonprofit/social-impact vertical is not among OpenAI's six Codex verticals (analytics, creative, sales, product design, equity investing, IB). Switching costs cited include decades of donor history and tax-exempt accounting workflows.
discoveries.thesis: BLKB serves the nonprofit/foundation/education vertical with embedded donor management workflows, tax-exempt accounting rules, and 3-year subscription contracts (~40% renewing in 2026). OpenAI's Codex verticals don't touch nonprofit fundraising workflows, donor data privacy, or HIPAA-adjacent compliance. The sympathy drop appears driven by 'vertical SaaS = AI roadkill' narrative rather than any actual Codex exposure.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stable renewal rates in the next 10-Q; continued growth in transaction-based payment processing fees; BLKB's own agentic AI features gaining adoption without margin compression.
discoveries.what_kills: Customer concentration shift, an unexpected renewal cliff in 2026 (40% of contracts renew), or a major nonprofit-focused AI competitor with vertical compliance know-how.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Vertical SaaS names with deep regulatory/data-history moats sell off on every OpenAI consumer release regardless of whether the new product touches their vertical. Track whether retail differentiates between 'vertical AI threat' and 'thematic SaaS panic.'
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: BLKB is software/SaaS and gets swept into 'AI replaces SaaS' selling, but its nonprofit vertical is outside the six Codex verticals OpenAI named.
research_pointers: Pull Q1 FY26 net revenue retention rate and compare to historical trend
Quantify the 2026 renewal cliff (40% of contracts) and read CC transcripts for renewal commentary
Check whether BLKB's own agentic AI launch is driving any ARPU expansion

discoveries.name: Tyler Technologies, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors emphasize 'long and complex sales cycles' with public sector clients, 'political resistance to the concept of contracting with third parties,' bid-procurement processes, and decades-long municipal/court records relationships. AI is discussed as an opportunity TYL is incorporating, not as a displacement threat.
discoveries.thesis: Tyler sells court records, municipal, and public-sector software via multi-year RFP-driven procurements. None of OpenAI's six Codex verticals (analytics, creative, sales, product design, equity investing, IB) target government records of record. No government CIO is replacing court case management with an LLM. The drop is clean thematic sympathy with no concurrent negative catalyst, and the move size sits squarely in the panic-fade zone.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued municipal RFP wins through Q2; stable SaaS ARR growth; no concentrated customer churn in next 10-Q.
discoveries.what_kills: A state or large municipal customer publicly piloting an LLM-based alternative for records-of-record; a major contract loss; impairment on convertible note settlement obligations.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Government-vertical workflow software has the strongest sympathy-fade asymmetry because procurement cycles are measured in years and switching cost is bureaucratically immense — yet retail treats it like any other SaaS on AI news days.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: TYL is application software and gets pulled in by the 'SaaS is AI-exposed' thematic, but its public-sector customer base and FedRAMP/state compliance moat is the antithesis of a Codex-vertical disruption target.
research_pointers: Check Q2 RFP win rate and bookings disclosure
Track any municipal AI procurement RFPs that compete with Tyler's existing footprint
Monitor convertible note maturity (March 2026) settlement mechanics

discoveries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Filings explicitly note MANH is incorporating generative and agentic AI into its WMS products and identifies AI as a development opportunity. The core business is enterprise WMS deeply embedded in physical warehouse operations — not analytics, creative, sales, design, equity investing, or IB.
discoveries.thesis: Manhattan's WMS sits inside physical warehouse infrastructure with multi-year ERP integrations. OpenAI's six Codex verticals don't intersect with supply chain execution or WMS. The sympathy drop is purely thematic SaaS panic. Moat is customer switching cost on enterprise-grade physical operations, not text generation.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stable cloud subscription growth; continued large-customer WMS deployments; AI features driving upsell rather than competitive displacement.
discoveries.what_kills: A major retail customer pilot of a competing AI-native WMS; cloud subscription deceleration tied to AI-driven price pressure.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Enterprise application software swept into SaaS thematic selling, despite zero overlap with the named Codex verticals.
research_pointers: Look at cloud RPO growth in Q1 FY26
Track customer concentration in retail vertical given retail industry disruption commentary

discoveries.name: Paylocity Holding Corporation
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors emphasize state-by-state payroll tax compliance, ACH banking relationships, HIPAA for benefits administration, and regulatory changes in tax/benefits/wage law. AI is discussed as both opportunity and competitive risk, but the moat is regulatory compliance rather than UI/UX.
discoveries.thesis: Payroll/HR compliance software requires filing 941s, handling state UI registrations, and maintaining banking relationships for ACH — none of which OpenAI's Codex verticals address. The drop is thematic SaaS panic and the move size is in the clean sympathy-fade zone with no concurrent negative catalyst.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stable client retention; recurring revenue growth in line with guidance; new client wins maintained in Q3 (fiscal year-end peak).
discoveries.what_kills: A major payroll competitor demonstrating successful AI-driven cost takeout that compresses Paylocity pricing; client retention deterioration.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: HR/payroll SaaS swept into thematic AI-replaces-back-office narrative despite regulatory compliance moats orthogonal to Codex verticals.
research_pointers: Check Q3 FY26 client retention and revenue retention disclosures
Track competitive wins/losses vs Paycom and ADP for AI feature differentiation

discoveries.name: Evolent Health, Inc
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors center on performance-based risk-sharing arrangements, oncology cost trends, partner concentration (Molina, Centene, Florida Blue, Cook County), and Medicaid policy risk — not AI competitive displacement.
discoveries.thesis: EVH's product is essentially payer risk-sharing contracts wrapped in software, with specialty care management for oncology/cardiology. OpenAI's Codex verticals don't touch payer risk arrangements. However, EVH has real operational headwinds (oncology cost overruns, customer concentration, OBBBA Medicaid risk) that may be driving part of the move — hence partial rather than unjustified.
discoveries.what_confirms: No partner attrition announcement in next earnings; oncology cost trends stabilizing.
discoveries.what_kills: Material partner non-renewal; further oncology cost overruns; OBBBA-related Medicaid membership decline.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Healthcare value-based-care names get swept into AI sympathy fades despite the actual business risks being operational/political. Confidence drops when the underlying business has independent stress.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Health IT services dragged in by 'AI replaces healthcare workflow software' thematic, but value-based-care contract moats are unrelated to Codex.
research_pointers: Read latest commentary on oncology cost trend and Performance Suite margin
Check partner concentration and any reprocurement risk

discoveries.name: nCino, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors include extensive AI/ML discussion, noting competitors increasingly adopting AI/ML and the explicit risk that 'systems internally developed by FIs leveraging large language models' could compete. However, the moat is OCC/FDIC examination requirements, multi-year FI implementations, and core banking integration.
discoveries.thesis: nCino's loan origination platform is embedded in bank regulatory workflows (OCC, FDIC examinations) and Salesforce-based core banking integrations. While the 10-K does acknowledge AI/ML competitive threats, OpenAI Codex isn't issuing banking charters or passing regulatory exams. The 5-day move is already extended, suggesting some of the panic is real, but a clean sympathy drop on the trigger day with no concurrent negative catalyst makes it OVERDONE.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued FI customer wins; stable subscription revenue growth; AI features driving upsell on the new asset-based pricing model.
discoveries.what_kills: FI customer publicly announcing in-house LLM replacement for loan origination; deterioration in renewal rates; failure of asset-based pricing model to gain traction.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When the candidate's own 10-K explicitly names LLMs as a competitive threat, threat_assessment shifts from minimal to indirect even when the regulatory moat is real — confidence drops accordingly.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Banking workflow software is conceptually adjacent to OpenAI's IB Codex vertical, even though community/regional banking origination is operationally distinct from IB workflows.
research_pointers: Read Q1 FY27 commentary on AI competition and customer adoption of nCino's own AI features
Track FI customer concentration and renewal rates
Monitor competitive RFP outcomes vs internally-built bank solutions

skipped.reason: Move (-14.93%) and 5-day (-68%) are far too large for a sympathy fade — this is a real impairment story (NYCDOT contract change, Ontario ban, Commercial Services renewal risk explicitly called out in 10-Q). Not pure sympathy.
Positive move (+8.72%); semi-cap process-control is a direct AI capex beneficiary as curation predicted, but the screen looks for negative panic moves to fade.

10-K explicitly names generative AI as a direct competitive threat to language learning ('translation services and products...people may generally become less interested in language learning'). Threat assessment is direct — real impairment risk, not pure sympathy fade.
Positive move (+5.98%); AI capex beneficiary, not a fade candidate.
Positive move (+5.39%); cybersecurity is benefiting from the AI-enhanced threat narrative.
Positive move (+3.98%); photomask supplier benefits from any chip production. Note 5-day -39% suggests prior independent stress, but today is not a sympathy fade.
Positive move (+3.58%); edge-AI SoC supplier is a direct beneficiary.
